preamble: The debuccalization of the voiceless lateral fricative [ɬ] into a glottal fricative [h] is a form of lenition where the oral place of articulation is lost, leaving only the laryngeal configuration.

examples.note: Documented historical shift where lateral fricatives are reduced to glottal fricatives in certain dialects.
Systematic debuccalization across several branches of the family.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
